key_insight: PBE-trained machine-learning potentials can incorrectly predict HfO2’s ground state, overstabilizing a low-density I41/amd phase over the experimentally observed monoclinic P21/c structure.
engineering_relevance: For crystal-structure screening and phase-transition modeling, the choice of DFT functional used in MLIP training can directly determine whether predicted energies and barriers are reliable.
method: Compared PBE-based MLIP predictions against known HfO2 phase ordering.
Cross-checked results with multiple DFT functionals, including PBEsol and LDA.
Examined ferroelectric switching paths to see how large lattice relaxations affect energy landscapes.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that both a custom PBE-based MLIP and widely used foundation models (NequIP-OAM-L, MatterSim-v1-5M) predicted I41/amd as more stable than P21/c, and that this error was traced to PBE energetics.
watch_item: Models trained on PBE data may inherit systematic phase-stability bias, so phase diagrams and barrier heights should be validated with alternative functionals or higher-fidelity calculations.
paper_title: A wrong ground-state structure of HfO$_2$ predicted by machine-learning interatomic potentials based on the PBE functional
